entities.role: Local planning authority
entities.quote: On December 13, 1950, the Philadelphia City Planning Commission having examined and studied the Eastwick Area, found that the area presented the following characteristics which warranted its being considered a Redevelopment Area as defined in Section 3(n) of the Pennsylvania Urban Redevelopment Law of May 24, 1945; P.L. 991
entities.stance_basis: Certified Eastwick as a Redevelopment Area, supporting the urban renewal project

entities.role: State environmental regulator monitoring project
entities.quote: Since late in 1974, the Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Resources (DER) has been monitoring procedures and activities in the project area.
entities.stance_basis: Actively monitoring erosion problems and requiring submission of a Soil Erosion Control Plan as condition of continued activity

entities.role: Federal agency that prepared AHSL Final EIS
entities.quote: The Final Environmental Statement for the AHSL prepared by the U.S. Department of Transportation, states "Acoustical shielding and landscaping will be used between Island Avenue and 88th Street to protect the proposed housing and school development from noise impact."
entities.stance_basis: Supports AHSL project with condition that acoustical shielding and landscaping be provided to mitigate noise impacts on housing

entities.entity: Department of Housing and Urban Development
entities.role: Responsible Federal Agency / EIS preparer
entities.quote: This Department will consent to the continuation of development within the residential portions of the Eastwick area which are subject to flooding with the understanding that funding for the proposed dike and surge basin improvements will be secured by the Redevelopment Authority and/or other City agencies, and, provided further, that construction of these essential facilities will be completed within three years of the release of this Impact Statement (February 1976).
entities.stance_basis: Consents to continued development only if flood control infrastructure is funded and built within three years

entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: The Redevelopment Authority should consult with appropriate state and federal agencies during early planning stages in an effort to develop the most environmentally acceptable plan.
entities.stance_basis: requests additional discussion of pollution/sedimentation plans and agency consultation before proceeding

entities.role: Deputy Assistant Secretary for Environmental Affairs, U.S. Department of Commerce
entities.quote: We believe that the statement should discuss which plans are being considered. Further, the Redevelopment Authority should consult with appropriate state and federal agencies during early planning stages in an effort to develop the most environmentally acceptable plan.
entities.stance_basis: conditions support on additional environmental planning and agency consultation

entities.entity: United States Department of Transportation, Federal Highway Administration
entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: No significant comments were made by U.S. DOT. Substantive comment was received and considered from PennDOT.
entities.stance_basis: no significant comments from a highway standpoint

entities.role: Division Administrator, Federal Highway Administration Region Three
entities.quote: We have reviewed the draft Environmental Impact Statement for the Eastwick Urban Renewal Area in Philadelphia. We have no significant comments from a highway standpoint.
entities.stance_basis: no significant comments on the project from highway perspective

entities.entity: Department of Army, Philadelphia District, Corps of Engineers
entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: Chapter VI. Flood plain management and rehabilitation to natural marsh conditions should also be considered as viable alternatives.
entities.stance_basis: raises multiple deficiencies in the EIS and recommends flood plain management alternatives be considered

entities.role: Chief, Engineering Division, Philadelphia District Corps of Engineers
entities.quote: Statement does not document fact sources.
entities.stance_basis: identifies multiple deficiencies requiring correction before the EIS is adequate

entities.entity: United States Department of the Interior, Office of the Secretary
entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: we urge that this open space be designated for park and recreation purposes, and to enhance the adjacent Tinicum Preserve. The final statement should contain evidence of further consideration and consultation with city officials on this possibility.
entities.stance_basis: urges park designation for open space and immediate soil erosion control plan as conditions

entities.role: Special Assistant to the Secretary, U.S. Department of the Interior, Northeast Region
entities.quote: we urge that this open space be designated for park and recreation purposes, and to enhance the adjacent Tinicum Preserve. The final statement should contain evidence of further consideration and consultation with city officials on this possibility.
entities.stance_basis: conditions support on park designation, erosion control plan, and historic preservation compliance

entities.entity: United States Environmental Protection Agency, Region III
entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: EPA has concerns about certain of those impacts on air quality where applicable standards may be exceeded: the final statement must quantify these impacts and specify mitigating measures in further detail in order to adequately demonstrate compliance.
entities.stance_basis: classified EIS as ER-2 (Environmental Reservations) and requires air quality quantification and mitigation measures

entities.role: Chief, EIS and Wetlands Review Section, EPA Region III
entities.quote: EPA has concerns about certain of those impacts on air quality where applicable standards may be exceeded: the final statement must quantify these impacts and specify mitigating measures in further detail in order to adequately demonstrate compliance.
entities.stance_basis: conditions adequacy of EIS on quantification of air quality impacts and specification of mitigation

entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: It is, therefore, our opinion that further continuance of the proposed development be halted until such time as an effective land use compatibility program is utilized or, at a minimum, an effective method of noise control.
entities.stance_basis: recommends halting development due to residential placement within noise-impacted airport zones

entities.role: Chief, Planning Staff, Federal Aviation Administration Eastern Region
entities.quote: It is, therefore, our opinion that further continuance of the proposed development be halted until such time as an effective land use compatibility program is utilized or, at a minimum, an effective method of noise control.
entities.stance_basis: opposes continued development due to noise incompatibility with airport operations

entities.entity: Urban Mass Transportation Administration, Region III
entities.role: federal reviewing agency
entities.quote: Our comments are confined to the transportation implications of the project. The lack of comments on other sections should in no way be interpreted as UMTA approval of the entire project.
entities.stance_basis: limits comments to transportation implications without approving or opposing the overall project

entities.role: Regional Director, UMTA Region III
entities.quote: thus, in assessing the RDA's project, the route #36 extension should be considered only a marginal possibility.
entities.stance_basis: clarifies transit status without taking a position on the overall project

entities.role: state reviewing agency
entities.quote: The proposed action will have no adverse impact on our existing and planned transportation facilities in the area.
entities.stance_basis: no adverse transportation impact but requests retention of I-695 right-of-way and additional air/noise data

entities.role: PennDOT District 6-0 engineer, commenter
entities.quote: We do want the right of way retained. We would not at all be reluctant to having the area provisionally used for wildlife management related operations. Any such use however we would want made emphatically clear to all as being provisional on our future use as a transportation corridor.
entities.stance_basis: conditions acceptance of wildlife use of I-695 ROW on it being explicitly provisional for future transportation use

entities.entity: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Department of Environmental Resources
entities.role: state reviewing agency
entities.quote: The environmental consequences of the continued expansion of the Eastwick Urban Renewal Area, as proposed by the Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority (P.R.A.) to the United States Department of Housing and Urban Development (H.U.D.) raise serious questions as to the advisability of funding further development without a great deal more study of ways to ameliorate the adverse impacts and without perhaps radical changes in the planning.
entities.stance_basis: raises serious questions about advisability of further development without additional study and radical planning changes

entities.role: Secretary of Environmental Resources, Pennsylvania DER
entities.quote: The environmental consequences of the continued expansion of the Eastwick Urban Renewal Area, as proposed by the Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority (P.R.A.) to the United States Department of Housing and Urban Development (H.U.D.) raise serious questions as to the advisability of funding further development without a great deal more study of ways to ameliorate the adverse impacts and without perhaps radical changes in the planning.
entities.stance_basis: conditions support on extensive additional study, erosion control, flood plain resolution, and possible radical planning changes

entities.entity: City of Philadelphia, City Representative and Director of Commerce
entities.role: local reviewing agency
entities.quote: The comment on additional planting to buffer the residential and industrial areas has been incorporated as a recommendation for consideration.
entities.stance_basis: recommends additional planting buffer and evaluation of Stage III ecological use

entities.entity: City of Philadelphia, Managing Director's Office
entities.role: local reviewing agency
entities.quote: The section on air quality has been rewritten and the Environmental Protection Agency, Region III has assisted in the assessment of air quality which is incorporated in this Final EIS.
entities.stance_basis: provided air quality data for incorporation; no explicit opposition or support stated

entities.role: local reviewing agency
entities.quote: This Final Impact Statement addresses the comments contained in the Planning Commission's letter.
entities.stance_basis: submitted comments addressed in final EIS; no explicit stance on project approval

entities.entity: Redevelopment Authority of the City of Philadelphia
entities.role: project applicant/local agency
entities.quote: The recommendation of the Authority is considered valid.
entities.stance_basis: project proponent supporting renewal plan and flood section recommendations

entities.role: local reviewing agency
entities.quote: The comment relative to the accuracy of the Draft EIS is noted.
entities.stance_basis: noted accuracy concerns without taking a position on the project

entities.role: major residential developer, commenter
entities.quote: The positive position taken with respect to the renewal plan is noted.
entities.stance_basis: explicitly noted as taking a positive position on the renewal plan

entities.role: community/project area committee, commenter
entities.quote: Most of the comments contained in the letter from the Project Area Committee have been addressed in the rewritten Sections of Air Quality, Noise and Flooding.
entities.stance_basis: raised concerns about sewage discharge, construction schedules, and city services for existing residents

entities.role: General Manager, SEPTA
entities.quote: we believe that the report in general accurately states SEPTA's long range policy for surface transportation in Eastwick.
entities.stance_basis: Confirms EIS accurately reflects SEPTA policy without objection or endorsement of the project itself

entities.role: Redeveloper for Stages I, II, III and IV
entities.quote: we feel it important to note that all development that has occurred has been as the result of the cooperation between the Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority, City Planning Commission, Philadelphia City Council, and the Department of Housing and Urban Development.
entities.stance_basis: Supports project continuation but raises multiple conditions regarding noise abatement costs, land use changes, and flood remedies

entities.role: Representative of New Eastwick Corporation, redeveloper
entities.quote: Stage III - As indicated earlier, consideration of an alternate land use for Stage III has been underway since 1969. As noted in the draft statement, plans for development of an alternate use have been submitted, verifying our endorsement of the plan change.
entities.stance_basis: Formally endorses land use change for Stage III to Airport-Related Commercial

entities.role: Redeveloper
entities.quote: New Eastwick Corporation and The Eastwick Joint Venture formally stated support for a proposed land use change and requested the Redevelopment Authority Board to begin the plan amendment process.
entities.stance_basis: Formally stated support for land use change for entire Stage III

entities.role: Community representative body for Eastwick neighborhoods
entities.quote: They are offered with the concern that the quality of life in the future Eastwick community is directly related to the attention of the agencies involved...to the abatement of potential negative environmental factors. The comments are offered, however, with the genuine interest of effectuating such planning and construction techniques as may mitigate these negative environmental factors now, in the interest of continued progress towards the completion of the Eastwick Project.
entities.stance_basis: Supports project completion but conditions it on mitigation of air, noise, water, and flooding problems

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association Elmwood Park Civic Association
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association Elmwood Park Civic Association Clearview Community Organization
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association Elmwood Park Civic Association Clearview Community Organization Penrose Park Civic Association
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association Elmwood Park Civic Association Clearview Community Organization Penrose Park Civic Association Conservation Area Towne Gardens Civic Association
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Member organization represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: REPRESENTING: Eastwick Community Organization Blue Bell Civic Association Elmwood Park Civic Association Clearview Community Organization Penrose Park Civic Association Conservation Area Towne Gardens Civic Association Eastwick Businessmens' Association
entities.stance_basis: Listed as represented by Eastwick Project Area Committee, which takes a conditional stance on the project

entities.role: Local redevelopment agency
entities.quote: The Redevelopment Authority of Philadelphia has been conspicuous in its disregard for the noise and air pollution impact of its activities upon the existing Eastwick Community.
entities.stance_basis: Criticized by Eastwick Project Area Committee for disregarding noise/air pollution impacts and poor coordination of development activities

entities.role: President, Eastwick Project Area Committee
entities.quote: We hope these remarks are useful in the completion of the Environmental Impact Statement for the Eastwick Project. We suggest, again, that the comments are offered for the record with the intention of encouraging timely resolution of the potential environmental problems of the Eastwick Project so that progress may continue.
entities.stance_basis: Supports project progress contingent on resolution of environmental problems identified in comments
